transcript.text: Probably a month out for...
meeting is being recorded.
Heads up, she'll probably drop into the external channel today.
That sounds good.
Can't believe it's been a year already.
I know, that's wild.
Yeah.
I'm it doesn't feel like a year.
Yeah.
I mean, it feels like...
It feels like...
It's weird, it's like the paradox of startup.
You know, it feels like one...
There's multiple years packed in one year, but at the same time, like, time is flying, so...
Yeah.
I couldn't agree more.
It's kind of what I...
This is totally stolen valor on my part, because I'm not a parent, but I feel like it's what people say about raising kids, where it's like, the days are long, but the years are short.
Like, that's how I feel.
That's exactly true.
You don't see yourself aging, but you see them growing.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Oh, that's a lovely way of thinking about it, actually.
Yeah.
Well, cool.
I also have some things that are a couple of...
So it's for more renewal-related, like what we would imagine for the next year of the partnership.
Also just kind of want to get your take on what priorities are.
I would be happy to start with that, and then we can kind of jump into performance.
But also let me know if you'd prefer to find, like, separate time.
I would just love to get you some things to consider maybe by the end of the week.
Yeah, I think it might be better to do a separate meeting for that.
Totally.
And that way I can also prepare a bit more.

like, if you want to send, like, a bit of an agenda, like what you're hoping to discuss, you know, what's the ask in terms of – because I understand, like, we come up with ideas and, like, new work streams.
I think it'd be interesting to understand, like, from – in terms of the dependencies you have with the engineering teams.
seems like, you that's a bit of a bottleneck in some cases.
So, I'm to understand, like, what's your, you know –
And then that way I can prepare and think about how we, you know, what we want to prioritize in the year and how that aligns with the rest of the company strategy as we plan for 2026.
Yeah, that makes perfect sense.
I'll share today with you just like in text kind of the outline of what I have.
And then if you have any feedback or things that you would like to look a little bit different for the conversation later this week, then I can definitely do that.
But do you have availability on maybe like Thursday?
Does that work for you?
I can also, if you have a Calendly, take a look.
Yeah, I have a Calendly.
It's cal.com slash, I'll just drop you the link.
Cool.
But it's Friday would be better for me.
Sure.
Paul, I feel like every time you join, it's a slightly different camera view.
It's exciting.
Yeah.
I don't know that he can hear us at this point.
Hey, Golzar, hey, CEO, hey, Paul.
Paul, can you hear us?
Hi, everyone.
Hey.
Was having small technical difficulties, but I'm good now.
Okay.
Don't worry.
We love the camera effects.
It's still looking good.
That's actually my actual background.
This is?
It's beautiful.
Yeah.
I'll fix it.
When I wake up, I like nice lights, but I've got my streaming setup, so here we go.
Fancy.
white lights.
Awesome.
Well, thanks for sending your Calendly, Victor.
I'll find some time, and then, Vivek, we can probably just jump into the regular programming.
Cool.
Let me share my screen real quick.

Okay, so just running through the updates, we delivered eight articles last week, and we have seven scheduled for this week, and one of those is the SendGrid integration page, so we'll have that out as well.
Just walking through the outstanding issues, so the first one was the widening comparison page column.
So I think last time we discussed, we were proceeding with the competitor versus competitor comparison pages, so I guess we can address this when those pages are done.
The integration page workflow, updating the meta information for these pages, this workflow is done, so we're currently executing that workflow, just updating it for all pages.
We expect it to be done by tomorrow, but generally the workflow works well, so there's no delays over there.
And then the CMS staging workflow, we're still having.
Some issues with the formatting, as Theo pointed out.
We're working through it.
It seems to be a bit inconsistent at times.
But again, as I mentioned last week, this workflow is pretty much automated.
It just needs a few finishing touches on our end just to make sure everything is going well.
The medium workflow is done.
Theo had provided feedback as well and clarified this in Slack.
So I think we're pretty good to go there.
What we'll do is from next week on, or rather at the end of this week, we can send over a list of URLs that we're planning on publishing.
And then you can let us know whether there's any priority there or whatever the timeline needs to be in that regard.
And then the social workflow right now, I think we kind of touched upon this in the beginning of this call.
But given, you know, just the engineering priorities, it is a bit of a bottleneck right now.
So, and just given the delays that...
That will occur in terms of creating this workflow for Strapi.
feel like it might be delayed a bit and it might not serve your needs, but we will definitely prioritize this and revisit this.
And we will keep you updated in terms of when that bottleneck clears and we can start working on this.
The next issues are the, this is the content gap.
And again, the, unfortunately, the messaging is the same here because it demands integration into Kappa and Octolens, which are tools specific to Strapi.
There's a bit of a bottleneck there in terms of the other workflows that engineering has prioritized.
So we will be revisiting this in a few weeks with engineering just to see if there's any room to take this on.
So, meanwhile, of course, we are.
And that, you know, specifically that are preventing you from integrating, because I thought you guys already have access to Kappa AI.
And in terms of Octolens, it's just a web.
That calls, that gives you access to the data.
mean, I, yeah.
There's no, yeah, there's, there's no technical difficulty.
It's more that we, the engineering team has prioritized a bunch of other workflows and creation in terms of the product.
So that's why there's no room to take it on.
Technically, I don't anticipate there being any issue.
And that's kind of why we want to revisit this one because it seems like something we can get done quickly.
So, yeah.

And also, just to jump in there, that might be something that, like, Victor, you and I chat through on Friday of just, like, I need to understand what the likelihood is of the team having the capability to, like, fast track that.
But we can discuss, like, how high of a priority that is and if there's a way to factor that in to maybe next year's work together.
But, yeah.
So a little bit more update then.
Yeah.

Well, just for me, like, the, it seems like we, we've been kind of, like, um, Mm-hmm.nosticolelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelelele...
E
Moving a bit slowly on some of the other works, you know, workflow and dependency with the engineering team.
You know, we've been working on the image generation one, like, it's been like six months.
And the result is not, you know, it's not like mind-blowing.
It is, you know, we're getting there, but it took us a long time to get there.
So, you know, I want to also put things into perspective that it's, you know, it feels like we're adding a lot, but we also, a lot of stuff has been kind of stuck.
So, that's my perspective.

So, I think we, it's not helping to understand where it was the true status of where things stand.
Yeah.
And, you know, to just give you visibility on what I consider priority, I think, like, there's some stuff that I feel like it's table steak, like the, you
Integration with Strapi, like that's something that we used to have with like Air Ops.
And I think when you guys moved to Atlas, we also, so there was like a bit of a step back.
So like also that one I consider as like, you know, kind of table stake and we've kind of been waiting on that to get fixed.
The medium is like, that's like a bonus.

I think it's like a nice to have, you know, it's obviously saves time for Theo.
So I think, you know, that's why we, and I feel like we, it was a bit harder than anticipated, but I wouldn't spend more time on, on the medium workflow that again, not strategic.
I think at this point, like what strategic is, is really looking at the, in particular, the integration with Kappa.
I think that's more than Octolance.
Like I think that's, that's also nice to have, but I think Kappa is a very important tool in, in, in the workflow, a different step of the workflow and we already use it.
It's a fact-checker in the fact-checking step.
But I think now it's even more relevant to use it for the discovery step.
And so, yeah, I think to me that I see that definitely as strategic.
We should think about it as it's more going to be content that is middle of the funnel, as in it will be strapi-related, right?
Because there are the content gaps within strapi.
And we want to spend most of our time at the top of the funnel.
think about GrowthX as top-of-the-funnel acquisition, like in terms of impression is what matters, non-branded ranking.
And so I think I want to talk about how we think about the distribution between top-of-the-funnel content and middle-of-the-funnel content.
Which is not, I think we don't have a very clear guidance at this time on what's the ratio in terms of volume and where we spend our time.
Does help a little bit?
Yeah, it definitely does.
Okay.

Okay, and then, yeah, just bringing up the image, Jen, we shared the initial output on there, Victor, regarding the company logos, cetera, adding data, I can take that back to the team and check how it's done.
Basically, at this point, we're running into, I think, just limitations of the LLM itself in terms of, like, what image it can generate.
So certainly, we'll take that back, and I'll let them know of your feedback as well.
For now, in terms of infographics, et cetera, we'll start using that flow to generate images within the article itself.

So that's pretty much it, and in terms of the social workflow, following up with Jason, followed up with him, and then, you know, the next step was basically what I described before, which was, like, we decided that the engineering workflow, sorry.
The engineering workload is a little blocked at the moment, so that's why we're going to revisit that shortly and see if we can get that up and running.
So those are the action items, and of course, I'll update all of these dates as well just to make sure that it reflects current timelines.
So, yeah, any questions or anything outstanding over here that you'd like to address?
Yeah, so I want to make sure we're good on the comparator pages because I'm a bit confused with the different updates.
Yeah, so FAQ is done.
Like, that's like, Theo, you should have FAQ available as a component, so that should no longer be a blocker.
What's the left, what's holding us up from like a website standpoint?
I think from our end, we've staged all these Trapi versus pages.
There was that one issue with the WordPress page.
Usman was updating that regularly.
sooo.
Don't Undercie?
But for some reason, it kept reverting to the old content.
I think for Yusman checked it yesterday, and he reported that the page was updated with the new content.
So from our end, the elements are in place, and the FAQs as well, we've been adding them as, I think, I forget the exact name of the element, but we have been adding them to the page.
So that's where we stand.
And of course, we're going ahead with the competitor versus competitor pages.
When you say staging, what's...

So if we use that as a definition, I think there was like six updated competitor...
...competitor...
Posts in staging.
Okay.
In draft.

But you cannot have a draft version of a published entry.
And so you have, so then it creates like URL mismanagement, right?
Because like we have, if we create like a new entry and of an existing entry, that's a duplicate.
And then we need to do a redirect.
So let me rephrase that.
So like the way we looked at it, we looked at updated by GrowthX.
So any comparator page that has, when I say draft, let me play Friday Father, it's the type, like it was updated last and it's by the GrowthX team and the date updated by.

Okay.
So, but how are they like, they're not.
So, yeah, that's the update from our end.
Okay.

But that happens on the front end, so it has nothing to do with the back end.
But I'll double check in terms of, like, competitors.
I think whenever the page gets published or updated, whoever is logged in, for instance, if they logged in and published with GrowthX, you're going to see that reflect on our site.
So I think it would be worth getting some clarity here because it seems like you only see six, but Vivek, you're saying you did a lot more.
So let's clarify.
It's been a while since we talked about those comparative pages.

I think at this point, I'd really like to kind of close this one as well and remove the big table, which, again, like we've been kind of waiting for everything to be published to remove the big table.
And but we.
Yeah.
So like to clarify, like now there's, you know, 15.
Hold on.
One, two, 10, 10.
Let me double check.
Actually, let me clarify here.
OK, so if we search by one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, so it's 17, 18, 19, 20, 21.
So 21 items.
I believe now we have, so yeah, so I did find the other entries and the total is 128.
So we have about 21 out of 128.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah, I think the rest of them are probably competitor versus competitor pages.
So those will be coming in the next few weeks.
Okay, perfect.
Thank you.
Yeah, strapi versus should be all done.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And so to me, like, then from there, like, we should really have like a pragmatic approach and looking at, okay, like from a traffic standpoint, like, what are like the top five?
And I, you know, it's probably going to be like strapi versus WordPress, strapi versus content for one, and make sure like those ones, we can make them as good as possible.

So like, I think that's where like, we can probably, Theo and Paul add some, you know, like for WordPress, I added like manually some stuff yesterday, because we just shipped some new case study that speak to like people that move from.
WordPress to Strapi and, like, a really nice video, really nice case study.
So I think that's the context that, you know, the team at GrowthX might not have because you don't see where everything else that's going on.
And that's where, like, I think, you know, Strapi team, we can optimize, again, like, for what's already in the top five.
Like, let's keep investing in, you know, making the pages even better since they're getting the bulk of the traffic.
Does that make sense?
Yeah, that makes sense.
Cool.
Okay, just, we're almost at time, so I'm just going to run through the performance report real quick.
So we're pretty close to previous highs in terms of, this is, of course, just content created by GrowthX.
These following pages were the top five this past week.
So basically, it's a bit of a change from the past few weeks.
Usually, we have a few integration pages in there, but, you know, people that So, have have projects, So, think
I can send this list to Fathom later on.
Non-branded impressions arose, which is good news.
It seems like Google has fixed the errors that were happening over the past few weeks, given the changes they had made.
Diving into cohort performance, so generally things look pretty good.
Most of our pillars are in the top 10, which is the last two columns in this image over here.
The second to last column is the actual position number, and the last column is the change compared to previous week.

So generally the refreshes that we have been targeting of the content that we've created, it's more about expanding the keywords that we're targeting, as opposed to doing a complete overhaul of the content to generate clicks from scratch.
So generally we seem to be doing quite well in terms of positions and clicks, et cetera.
In terms of conversion events this past week, pretty much all conversion events rose.
We just had a slight decline in pricing.
Link navigation clicks as well as contact sales.
In terms of going through HubSpot, new contact generation has been holding pretty steady.
And in terms of sessions as well, we are ahead of the goals as they're marked in HubSpot.
Of course, we would like to do better with the new contacts since that peak in April.
But generally, we seem to be heading in the right direction.
Looking at the LLM visibility, so Scrunch changed their charts a bit.
So that's why we now have a line chart instead of what was previously a bar chart.
But generally, Strapi's presence is highly competitive and positive compared to everybody else across all the prompts and queries that we are tracking.
Strapi is in first position ahead of competitors like Contentful, Sanity, WordPress, iGraph.
Strapi Citations have also increased over the past week.
So generally, LLM visibility is quite positive.
One of the sort of insights that Scrunch has given us is essentially there's this list of prompts.
So these prompts are the ones where strapi is not showing up, but competitors are showing up in ChatGPT.

So the idea is essentially what we're going to do is we're going to take these questions and essentially map them to existing content and see if we can include these as part of refreshes or content enrichment, et cetera.
The idea is to have these questions essentially as H2s, and we can create content for it and see whether the ChatGPT mentions improve.
So that's the action plan in terms of closing the LLM gap over there.
Any questions I can answer about any of this?
Yeah, Vivek, it be really nice to look at LLM traffic charts in this weekly report.
I want to see like...
Over time, you know, how that's moving.
I know we have that in Looker, but I think if we can add it on the radar every week, that'd be great.
Sure, I'll base that in.
Looker's being a bit slow right now, otherwise I would show it to you live.
But sure, I will include that as part of the post-call recap as well, moving forward.
Appreciate it.
And yeah, so that's kind of it.
So that's pretty much it for this week's update.
And yeah.
Okay, how are we doing in terms of the number of kind of the backlog of topics in Airtable?
Like, do we have like, I think we submitted some ideas.
You were also providing some ideas.
Yeah.
So the ideas that Paul and Theo had mentioned, we are pretty much, I think we'll be getting a I

The content priorities pipeline-wise are the comparison pages, so we're doing about two to three pages a week at this point, and then there was enough, based on our own research of the competition and also of the audience, there were quite a few topics as well, primed and ready to go.
So most of them are top funnel, as you mentioned, because they had some pretty strong keyword correlations and also, yeah, basically targeting top funnel traffic.
There are some topics in terms of, you know, explaining how to do something with Strapi.
We're kind of treating that as mid to bottom funnel content.
So generally, the content pipeline is pretty healthy, of course.
You know, if you see any gaps based on your internal research, please do send that on to us.
More than happy to include that.
But we're conducting research on our own as well.
So generally, things are pretty healthy there.
Sounds good.
Yeah, I think, like, at a high level, like, there's...

This theme, probably like a lot of your customers, but this theme for us in Q4 is AI and how like, so we have a lot going on there with, you we released beginning of October, like, Strapi AI, which is like doing like automated content type building and media management and then translation is coming up soon.
And so we have this kind of agents, content agents coming to Strapi.
also have like, we're launching a new product that could also benefit from, you know, more like content around that kind of AI cluster.
So I'm thinking like, we should really think about, okay, what, not necessarily showing up in the data yet, but what's showing up in, in terms of like, what's trending.
And that's a, it's more of a call, I think, call to action for, well, Strapi side than, than for growthx side.
Strapi
But I'm thinking, like, just to give you examples, like, a lot of people are using the Versal AI SDK, right?
And so, like, also you have OpenAI release, like, the app SDK.
So there's a lot happening in terms of, like, the kind of the plumbing to build those applications.

And I think, like, having listicles around to help folks, like, navigate through, like, okay, what's available and what are some of the things that are doing well and how that relates to CMSs, I think that, you know, that I see an opportunity there to get ahead of the curve.
It's not, it might not show up in the data already, but we know that's where the world and the industry is going.
Yeah, for sure.
We will look into that and propose a few topics in that regard, but for sure, yeah, totally on board with that.
Cool.
Okay, that was pretty much it.
I mean, will...
Follow up in Slack, I guess.
And yeah, thanks, everyone.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Thank you, everyone.
Bye.
Bye.
See you later.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Strapi and GrowthX teams to discuss content strategy, performance, and ongoing projects.](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=230.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content pipeline is healthy, with focus on comparison pages and top-funnel content](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1414.0)
  - [LLM visibility for Strapi is positive, outperforming competitors in tracked prompts](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)
  - [AI-related content opportunities identified as a priority for Q4, aligning with Strapi's recent product launches](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1497.0)
  - [Some engineering-dependent workflows (e.g., social, content gap analysis) are delayed due to prioritization](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)

## Topics

### Partnership Renewal Discussion

  - [Victor and Mara to have a separate meeting on Friday to discuss next year's partnership](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=56.0)
  - [Mara to share outline of discussion points in text format beforehand](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=133.0)
  - [Focus on aligning priorities and understanding engineering dependencies](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=89.0)

### Content Production Update

  - [8 articles delivered last week, 7 scheduled for this week](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
  - [SendGrid integration page to be published this week](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
  - [Integration page meta information update workflow is progressing well](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=276.0)

### Workflow Status

  - [Medium workflow is completed and ready for use](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=319.0)
  - [CMS staging workflow has some formatting issues but is largely automated](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=297.0)
  - [Social workflow and content gap analysis tools integration delayed due to engineering priorities](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)

### Comparison Pages Progress

  - [21 out of 128 comparison pages completed](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1034.0)
  - [Focus on optimizing top 5 traffic-driving pages (e.g., Strapi vs WordPress)](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1105.0)
  - [Team to take a pragmatic approach, enhancing high-traffic pages with recent case studies and updates](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1105.0)

### Performance Report Highlights

  - [Content performance close to previous highs](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1172.0)
  - [Non-branded impressions increased](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1172.0)
  - [Most content pillars in top 10 positions](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1213.0)
  - [Conversion events generally rose, with slight declines in pricing link clicks and contact sales](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1252.0)

### LLM Visibility

  - [Strapi maintains a competitive edge in LLM visibility across tracked prompts](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)
  - [Plan to address prompts where Strapi isn't showing up by mapping to existing content and refreshing](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1325.0)

### AI Content Strategy

  - [Q4 theme focuses on AI, aligning with Strapi's recent product launches (Strapi AI, content agents)](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1497.0)
  - [Opportunity identified for content around AI SDKs, plumbing for AI applications, and CMS relevance](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=1541.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Mara to schedule Friday meeting with Victor for partnership renewal discussion](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=56.0)
  - [Vivek to update project timelines in the tracking document](https://fathom.video/share/D4u7QZ9tDB3pMZmyKyUMU917ae8rNQMx?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
